sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) song, singing. 2Ch 29:28, מְשֹׁרֵר וְהַחֲצֹוצְרֹות מַהֲצֹצְרִים הַשִּׁיר “the song sang and the trumpets trumpeted,” i.e. the singing began, and the trumpets began to be blown; which is in verse 27 2Ch 29:27, הֵחֵל שִׁיר יְהֹוָה “the song of Jehovah began.” Kimchi gives an incorrect explanation of שִׁיר in this place, as בַּעַל שִׁיר a singer (it would have been better בַּעֲלֵי שִׁיר chorus of singers); and still less tolerable is the conjecture of Winer (Lex., p. 973), who thinks that “levi mutatione,” we may put מְשֹׁרֵר מְשֹׁרֵר (?). Also used of instrumental music. כְּלֵי שִׁיר instruments of music, 1Ch 16:42 2Ch 7:6 34:12 Amo 6:5 Neh 12:27, בְּשִׁיר מְצִלְתַּיִם “with music of cymbals.”

(2) a song, both sacred, Psa 33:3 40:4 (and so frequently), and profane, Isa 23:16 24:9 Ecc 7:5 Eze 33:32 especially joyful, Amo 8:10 (opp. to קִינָה). שִׁיר הַשִּׁירִים Son 1:1 Vulg. canticum canticorum, i.e. most excellent song, comp. the phrases, עֲדֵי עֲדָיִים (most beautiful ornament) Eze 16:7 שְׁמֵי הַשָּׁמַיִם (the highest heaven) 1Ki 8:27 which, as title of a book (hardly proceeding from the author himself [?]) contains a commendation similar to שִׁיר יְדִידֹות Psa 45:1.
